_comment: Viva Piñata franchise data. All media including defunct, cancelled, and inaccessible entries.
description: Rare's cozy garden-sim franchise (2006–2008): attract, romance, and protect candy-filled piñata creatures while fighting off Professor Pester. Four games, a 52-episode 4Kids animated series, an IDW comic run, children's books, and a full consumer products push — then went dormant.

media.title: Viva Piñata
media.description: The original garden-sim — attract 60 piñata species to your plot, romance them, and defend against Ruffians. Included in Rare Replay.
media.notes: Available on Xbox Game Pass and Rare Replay. Best entry point for historical context; Trouble in Paradise is the better game.

media.title: Viva Piñata (Windows / GFWL)
media.defunct_reason: GFWL activation broken on modern Windows. Online features and cloud saves permanently gone. Use Xbox 360 / Rare Replay version.
media.description: PC port of the original game, handled by Climax Group and distributed via Games for Windows Live.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: GFWL activation is broken on modern Windows installs. The core single-player game installs from disc but online features and achievements are gone. Use the Xbox 360 / Rare Replay version instead.

media.title: Viva Piñata: Party Animals
media.description: Minigame party collection featuring eight TV-series characters. Not a gardening game.
media.notes: Optional. Developed by Krome Studios, not Rare. Fun in local multiplayer; thin on content. Online now defunct.

media.title: Viva Piñata: Trouble in Paradise
media.description: The definitive VP game — adds Piñarctic and Dessert Desert biomes, co-op, Piñata Vision camera cards, and 100+ species.
media.notes: Best game in the franchise. Included in Rare Replay. Piñata-sharing servers are offline; Piñata Vision camera accessory is discontinued but fan-printed cards still work.

media.title: Viva Piñata: Pocket Paradise
media.description: Standalone handheld rework of the original game for Nintendo DS, published by THQ.
media.notes: Touch-screen controls. Some mechanics simplified vs the Xbox version (no Wildcard variants). The only entry playable on mobile via melonDS / EmulatorJS.

media.title: Viva Piñata (TV Series)
media.network: Fox (4Kids TV) S1 / CW4Kids S2 / YTV (Canada)
media.description: 4Kids/Bardel animated series set on Piñata Island, following Fergy Fudgehog, Hudson Horstachio, and friends in episodic 22-minute adventures. S1 (2006, 26 eps) aired on Fox's Saturday morning block; S2 (2007–2009, 26 eps) moved to CW4Kids and YTV. Final US broadcast Oct 2008; final YTV broadcast May 2009.
media.notes: Launched same week as the Xbox 360 game. Animations use the game's 3D character models. Episodes approved by Rare's Gregg Mayles for game accuracy. Full series archived on Internet Archive (vivapinatafullseries). Never released on DVD commercially. Series ended — not cancelled mid-run; completed its planned 52-episode order.

media.title: Viva Piñata (IDW Comics) #1–5
media.author: Ted Naifeh
media.defunct_reason: Series ended after 5 issues. Out of print. No digital release. Physical copies only via secondhand market.
media.description: Five-issue comic series by IDW Publishing (2009), written and illustrated by Ted Naifeh. Episodic stories set on Piñata Island featuring the TV-series cast. Targeted at the same 6–12 demographic as the animated show.
media.notes: DEFUNCT/OOP: Out of print. No digital release on Comixology or equivalent. Physical issues only — scarce on eBay and secondhand. Series concluded naturally at issue 5; not cancelled mid-arc. Tie-in to the TV series continuity, not the games directly.

media.title: Viva Piñata: Trouble in Paradise (Official Strategy Guide)
media.author: BradyGames
media.defunct_reason: Out of print. Physical copy only. Digital edition never released.
media.description: Official strategy guide covering all 88 piñata species, romance requirements, the Piñarctic and Dessert Desert zones, Piñata Vision card list, and complete garden management reference.
media.notes: OOP: Out of print. Physical copies surface secondhand. Comprehensive species/romance guide — still the most complete single reference for TiP. The companion VP1 guide (2006, Prima Games) is similarly out of print.

media.title: Viva Piñata (Official Strategy Guide)
media.author: Prima Games
media.defunct_reason: Out of print. Physical copy only.
media.description: Official Prima Games strategy guide for the original Xbox 360 game. Full species guide, gardening tips, and romantic requirements for all 60 piñata species.
media.notes: OOP: Out of print. Secondhand only. Predates Trouble in Paradise; does not cover TiP content.

media.title: Viva Piñata: Official Children's Storybook / Activity Books
media.author: Various
media.defunct_reason: Out of print. No confirmed full catalogue; titles produced under 4Kids licensing umbrella. Availability extremely limited.
media.description: A range of children's activity books, storybooks, and early-reader tie-ins produced under the 4Kids licensing programme (2006–2008). Targeted at the TV-series audience. Known titles include simple storybook adaptations of TV episodes and brand-illustrated colouring/activity books. Publisher varies by region (Scholastic in North America, Egmont in UK).
media.notes: OOP/UNCERTAIN: No comprehensive catalogue exists. Titles were produced as promotional licensing product tied to the TV run. Physical copies surface very rarely. Not tracked as a cohesive series — treat as ephemeral licensed print product.

media.title: 'Your Garden' Prototype (lost)
media.defunct_reason: LOST: No public build exists. Pre-dates the piñata concept entirely. Documented in developer retrospectives only.
media.description: Tim Stamper's original gardening game prototype for Pocket PC (later ported to original Xbox) before the piñata concept was adopted.
media.notes: LOST: No public build exists. Pre-dates the piñata concept entirely. Documented in developer retrospectives but no screenshots or playable version available.

media.title: Cancelled VP3 / Trouble in Paradise Sequel
media.defunct_reason: CANCELLED: Never entered production. Gregg Mayles stated the team lacked enough new gameplay concepts to justify a sequel. Never publicly announced.
media.description: A third mainline Viva Piñata game that was conceptually discussed but never entered production.
media.notes: CANCELLED: Gregg Mayles stated near TiP's release that the team had ideas but not enough new gameplay concepts to justify a sequel. Never announced publicly.

media.title: Viva Piñata for Nintendo 3DS (discussed)
media.defunct_reason: NEVER MADE: A Microsoft exec expressed interest in early 2007 but no development was commissioned. The franchise went dormant before a 3DS entry could be produced.
media.description: A Nintendo 3DS entry discussed by Microsoft and Rare during Pocket Paradise's development but never produced.
media.notes: NEVER MADE: A Microsoft exec expressed interest in early 2007. Pocket Paradise's commercial underperformance on NDS made a follow-up 3DS title a non-starter. Never formally announced.
